mnemonics.title: SWING Origins
mnemonics.content: AWT Limitations: Heavy (Heavyweight components), Ugly (Limited widgets), Tight (Platform-dependent). SWING Solves With: Lightweight, Widget-rich, Independent, Nice-looking, Graphical

mnemonics.title: Swing vs AWT
mnemonics.content: AWT = 'A Weighty Toolkit', Swing = 'Superior Widgets Invigorate New GUIs'

mnemonics.title: MVC in Swing
mnemonics.content: Model (Data), View (Display), Controller (Input) → Remember 'Madame VC Designs Interfaces'

acronyms.usage: Remembers Swing's lightweight nature replacing AWT's heavyweight components
Key Swing feature allowing UI style changes without code modification
Original Java GUI toolkit that Swing improved upon

visualTips.visualization: Imagine AWT components as concrete blocks (heavy, rooted to OS) vs Swing as paper cutouts (light, movable anywhere)
Picture a 3-layer sandwich: Data (bread), Display (lettuce), Control (tomato)
Visualize a chameleon - same component changing colors/skins for different OS appearances
Imagine a tree: javax.swing (trunk) with branches like swing.text (text), swing.table (tables), swing.plaf (skins)

analogies.analogy: Like actors wearing different costumes (Swing) vs hiring new actors for each role (AWT)
AWT is a fixed toolbox with limited tools. Swing is a modular workshop where you can create/add any tool
Like a restaurant: Model (kitchen/store), View (plated dish), Controller (waiter taking orders)
